req.title: Manager deletes training-course
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to delete a training-course through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers a manager-role UI delete action on /training-courses. I selected: (1) EP positive – a fully valid delete by an authenticated manager verifies the happy path, 200/201 response, entity removal, and audit record creation; (2) NEG negative – an unauthenticated request expects a 401 redirect/response; (3) DT negative – a forbidden role (employee) expects 403; (4) BVA boundary – deleting a course whose ID is at the numeric boundary (minimum valid ID = 1) ensures edge-case ID handling; (5) STC – verifies the state transition from 'existing' to 'deleted' including idempotency on a second delete attempt returning 404. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up data.

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_valid_training_course
preconditions: A training course with known ID exists in the system
actions: Select the target training course row
Click the Delete button and confirm the dialog
expected: Response body contains the deleted entity ID
Training course no longer appears in the course list
Audit record is written for the delete action
trace: R-WEB-047: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_deletes_valid_training_course(manager_page, create_training_course, audit_log_client):
    course_id = create_training_course(title="EP Delete Course")
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/training-courses")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-course-id='{course_id}']")
    row.locator("[data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()
    response = page.wait_for_response(
        lambda r: "/training-courses" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    )
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(course_id)
    expect(page.locator(f"[data-course-id='{course_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()
    audit = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=course_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not found"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_training_course
preconditions: No active session (user is not authenticated)
A training course with known ID exists
actions: Send DELETE request to /training-courses/{id} without authentication credentials
expected: Training course still exists in the system
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_training_course(base_url, create_training_course, audit_log_client):
    course_id = create_training_course(title="NEG Unauth Course", cleanup=True)
    url = f"{base_url}/training-courses/{course_id}"
    response = requests.delete(url)
    assert response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"
    audit = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=course_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None, "Audit record must not be written for unauthenticated request"
    verify = requests.get(url, headers=<admin_auth_headers>)
    assert verify.status_code == 200, "Course must still exist after rejected delete"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_training_course
preconditions: User is authenticated with role: employee (not manager)
A training course with known ID exists
actions: Attempt to delete a training course via the UI or API
expected: Delete button absent or disabled in UI
Training course still exists in the system
trace: R-WEB-047: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_delete_training_course(base_url, employee_auth_headers, create_training_course, audit_log_client):
    course_id = create_training_course(title="DT Forbidden Course", cleanup=True)
    url = f"{base_url}/training-courses/{course_id}"
    response = requests.delete(url, headers=employee_auth_headers)
    assert response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"
    audit = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=course_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None, "Audit record must not be written for forbidden role"
    verify = requests.get(url, headers=<admin_auth_headers>)
    assert verify.status_code == 200, "Course must still exist after rejected delete"

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_minimum_boundary_id_training_course
preconditions: A training course with the minimum valid numeric ID (1) exists or is seeded
actions: Target the course with ID=1 (minimum boundary)
Click Delete and confirm the dialog
expected: Response body contains id equal to 1
Course with ID=1 no longer appears in course list
Audit record written for entity id=1
trace: R-WEB-047: BVA on entity ID lower boundary (ID=1)
R-WEB-047: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_deletes_minimum_boundary_id_training_course(manager_page, seed_training_course_with_id, audit_log_client):
    course_id = seed_training_course_with_id(course_id=1, title="BVA Min ID Course")
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/training-courses")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-course-id='{course_id}']")
    row.locator("[data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()
    response = page.wait_for_response(
        lambda r: "/training-courses" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    )
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == "1", f"Expected entity id=1, got {body.get('id')}"
    expect(page.locator("[data-course-id='1']")).not_to_be_visible()
    audit = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=1, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not found for boundary ID=1"

tests.name: test_deleted_course_state_transition_to_not_found
preconditions: A training course exists and is in state: active
actions: Delete the training course (first delete — valid transition: active -> deleted)
Attempt to delete the same course again (second delete — invalid transition: deleted -> deleted)
expected: Second delete attempt returns HTTP 404 (resource no longer exists)
Only one audit record written (for the first delete)
trace: R-WEB-047: error pathway validation on already-deleted resource
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_deleted_course_state_transition_to_not_found(base_url, manager_auth_headers, create_training_course, audit_log_client):
    course_id = create_training_course(title="STC State Transition Course")
    url = f"{base_url}/training-courses/{course_id}"
    first = requests.delete(url, headers=manager_auth_headers)
    assert first.status_code in (200, 201), f"First delete: expected 200/201, got {first.status_code}"
    body = first.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(course_id)
    audit_first = audit_log_client.get_latest_entry(entity_id=course_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit_first is not None, "Audit record must exist after first delete"
    second = requests.delete(url, headers=manager_auth_headers)
    assert second.status_code == 404, f"Second delete: expected 404, got {second.status_code}"
    audit_count = audit_log_client.count_entries(entity_id=course_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit_count == 1, f"Expected 1 audit record, found {audit_count}"
